Title: MYB repressors and MBW activation complex collaborate to fine-tune flower coloration in Freesia hybrida

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB; subgroup 4 MYB; bHLH-interaction motif [D/E]Lx2[R/K]x3Lx6Lx3R; C1-like, C2-like, C5-like, and EAR repression motifs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation with ecological and ornamental value; pollinator attraction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation in Freesia petals; also reduces PA accumulation when ectopically expressed in Arabidopsis/tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong anthocyanin repressor that converts positive MBW activity into suppressive output via its C-terminal repression domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses anthocyanin biosynthetic genes activated by FhPAP1/FhTT8L; suppresses Fh3GT1 promoter activation; suppresses promoters of FhPAP1, FhTT8L, FhMYB27 and FhMYBx when coexpressed with activators
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression through interaction with bHLH-containing MBW complex; feedback repression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (ABGs), including Fh3GT1; regulatory promoters of FhPAP1; FhTT8L; FhMYB27; FhMYBx
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedback loop: activated by FhPAP1/MBW complex and then represses MBW-driven anthocyanin pathway output
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Flower developmental progression; activation by FhPAP1 alone or with FhTT8L/MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through the MBW complex by binding bHLH factor FhTT8L; transforms activator complex into repressor complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increases during flower development; highly expressed in colored petals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in petals; grouped with late biosynthetic genes and MBW components during floral development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; floral developmental stage strongly affects expression and anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; LexA-Gal4:GUS repression assay; Gal4:GUS transactivation assay; Freesia protoplast cotransfection; transient expression in Freesia petals; Arabidopsis and tobacco overexpression; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; promoter activation assays; ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic database mining of Freesia; sequence accessions MT210094
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic overexpression in Arabidopsis caused glabrous phenotype and transparent testa/low PA accumulation; in tobacco caused pale red or white flowers and reduced PAs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful negative regulator for fine-tuning flower color intensity/patterning in ornamentals; caution that strong overexpression may reduce desirable pigmentation and affect PA-related traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FhMYB27 encodes an anthocyanin-related R2R3-MYB repressor with conserved repression motifs. 2. It strongly suppresses MBW-activated anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression and reduces petal anthocyanin accumulation. 3. It interacts with FhTT8L and functions mainly through the MBW complex rather than direct promoter binding. 4. FhPAP1 directly activates the FhMYB27 promoter, supporting a feedback loop in anthocyanin regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FhMYB27 has stronger repression capacity than FhMYBx because of C-terminal repression motifs; C2/C5 motifs contribute partially and C-terminal deletion severely weakens repression; classified as FaMYB1-like repressor that does not directly bind target promoter in this system

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R3-MYB; bHLH-interaction motif [D/E]Lx2[R/K]x3Lx6Lx3R; lacks C-terminal repression motifs typical of R2R3 repressors
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation with ecological and ornamental value; pollinator attraction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation, but weaker and dosage-dependent compared with FhMYB27
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a moderate anthocyanin repressor mainly by titrating bHLH proteins and reducing positive MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Partially represses ABG expression activated by FhPAP1/FhTT8L; modestly suppresses Fh3GT1 promoter activation; suppresses promoters of FhPAP1, FhTT8L, FhMYB27 and FhMYBx when coexpressed with activators
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression indirectly through competitive sequestration of bHLH factor from MBW complex; feedback repression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MBW-controlled anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, including Fh3GT1; indirectly affects promoter activity of FhPAP1; FhTT8L; FhMYB27; FhMYBx
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedback loop: activated by FhPAP1/MBW complex and then attenuates MBW output
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Flower development; activation by FhPAP1 alone or with FhTT8L/MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interferes with MBW activation complex formation by titrating FhTT8L
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during flower development and high in petals, but also substantially expressed in non-colored tissues; highest transcript level among tested samples
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in petals and also abundant in non-colored tissues/organs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; floral developmental stage strongly affects expression and anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; LexA-Gal4:GUS repression assay; Gal4:GUS transactivation assay; Freesia protoplast cotransfection; transient expression in Freesia petals; Arabidopsis and tobacco overexpression; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; promoter activation assays; ChIP-qPCR for upstream activator binding to its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic database mining of Freesia; sequence accessions MT210095
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression in Arabidopsis caused reduced anthocyanin and glabrous phenotype; no obvious phenotype in tobacco, likely due to interaction specificity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for dosage-sensitive tuning of floral pigmentation and patterning with potentially milder effects than FhMYB27; activity may be species-context dependent because bHLH partner compatibility matters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FhMYBx encodes an anthocyanin-related R3-MYB repressor. 2. It partially suppresses MBW-induced anthocyanin gene expression and petal anthocyanin accumulation in a dosage-dependent manner. 3. It interacts with FhTT8L and mainly represses by competing for bHLH binding rather than using a repression domain. 4. Its promoter is activated by FhPAP1, placing it in a feedback regulatory loop.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FhMYBx lacks a C-terminal repression domain, and fusion to VP16 converts it into an activator; unlike FhMYB27, repression depends more on competitive bHLH sequestration; interaction with tobacco bHLH regulators was not supported, explaining weak heterologous phenotype in tobacco

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation with ecological and ornamental value; pollinator attraction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis by activating ABGs and regulatory genes in Freesia flowers
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive MYB activator driving anthocyanin pathway and initiating feedback regulation by inducing repressors
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ABGs including Fh3GT1; activates FhTT8L, FhPAP1, FhMYB27 and FhMYBx promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as MYB component of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; FhTT8L; FhPAP1; FhMYB27; FhMYBx
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive autoregulation and activation of downstream repressor genes create hierarchical and feedback loops
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Component of the MBW activation complex with FhTT8L and FhTTG1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Progressively expressed during flower development; highest in petals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in petals; increases with flower pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast cotransfection; transient Freesia petal expression; promoter activation assays; ChIP-qPCR; interaction assays from prior/current study integration
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate activator for enhancing floral anthocyanin, but may require balancing with repressors for controlled pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FhPAP1 acts as a central positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in Freesia. 2. Together with FhTT8L/FhTTG1 it activates ABGs. 3. It directly activates FhTT8L, FhMYB27 and FhMYBx promoters, establishing hierarchical and feedback control.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although characterized in prior studies, it is a core gene here because multiple Results experiments directly test its regulation of repressors and target promoters

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation with ecological and ornamental value; pollinator attraction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis as essential bHLH partner in MBW complex and bridge for repressor action
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Indispensable bHLH component required for positive MBW complex formation and for MYB repressors to exert inhibitory effects
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Co-activates ABGs with FhPAP1; its promoter is activated by FhPAP1/MBW; serves as direct interaction target for FhMYB27 and FhMYBx
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in complex with FhPAP1 and FhTTG1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback via activation of its own promoter by FhPAP1/MBW context; target of repressor sequestration
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Flower developmental progression; activation by FhPAP1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core bHLH bridge in MBW activation complex and in repressor-associated complexes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in petals; increases during flower development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in petals; concurrent with anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast cotransfection; transient Freesia petal assays; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; promoter activation assays; ChIP-qPCR for promoter activation by FhPAP1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key node for engineering anthocyanin intensity, but perturbation may be counteracted by MYB repressors that sequester or repurpose it
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FhTT8L is an essential positive bHLH component of the Freesia MBW anthocyanin complex. 2. It interacts with both FhMYB27 and FhMYBx, enabling repression. 3. Its promoter is activated by FhPAP1, contributing to hierarchical regulation and positive feedback.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central mechanistic bridge: FhMYB27 uses FhTT8L to convert MBW activity into repression, whereas FhMYBx titrates FhTT8L to reduce MBW formation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation with ecological and ornamental value; pollinator attraction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports anthocyanin biosynthesis as WD40 scaffold component of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Scaffold necessary for the positive MBW activation complex in Freesia anthocyanin regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports MBW-mediated activation of ABGs in complex with FhPAP1 and FhTT8L
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: WD40 scaffold of MBW activation complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling; interaction evidence from prior/current integrated MBW assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential MBW support gene for anthocyanin engineering, though less specific than MYB activator/repressors
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FhTTG1 is part of the Freesia MBW activator complex. 2. It is highly expressed in petals together with other anthocyanin regulators. 3. It supports anthocyanin activation in the regulatory network described in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results explicitly evaluate MBW network components and interactions, although major mechanistic focus is on MYB repressors and FhPAP1/FhTT8L.
